author: feral-core
description: FERAL's never-say-no escape hatch. Writes ad-hoc Python/Node/bash scripts to the user's ~/.feral/workspace/scripts/ folder, executes them in the Docker sandbox (or host with explicit consent), and keeps a reusable catalog. Use this whenever no existing skill fits the user's request — you can still deliver the result by generating a tiny script on the fly. The OpenClaw `exec` equivalent, but with persistence + reuse.

endpoints.description: Write code to ~/.feral/workspace/scripts/<uuid>.<ext> and execute it in the sandbox. Supports language in {python, bash, node}. Persistent — the script stays for reuse. On success, a catalog entry is recorded so future turns can reference it by name. Use this when no specialized skill fits but the task is expressible as a short program.

params.name: code
params.description: Complete source code. For python, print() your answer. For bash/node, echo / console.log.

params.name: name
params.description: Short human-friendly name for the catalog (e.g. 'csv_to_json'). Auto-generated if omitted.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional stringified JSON args forwarded to the script as env var FERAL_ARGS.

endpoints.description: List previously-saved scripts that succeeded. Each entry has id, name, language, path, args_schema (if provided). Use this before writing a new script — a previous one may already do the job.
endpoints.returns_description: {scripts: [{id, name, language, path, created_at, last_run, runs}]}

endpoints.description: Re-run a previously-saved script by id, optionally overriding args.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional stringified JSON args forwarded as FERAL_ARGS.

endpoints.description: Delete a saved script and its catalog entry.

params.name: script_id
params.description: Catalog id of the script to delete.
